rationale: Component-specific views need modeled local view surfaces.
Component-specific reports need modeled report surfaces.
Diagnostic storage/collection should be explicit.
Logging/telemetry should be export adapters, not core diagnostics.
Recovery behavior starts as classification and routing, not hidden control flow.
Capacity gap should be handled under a capacity profile subcomponent.
Horizontal scaling is a capacity/scaling profile concern.
Vertical scaling is family-wide capacity profile switching.
Container creation semantics should be modeled before behavior.
Container mutation should be mediated by state transitions.
Container storage needs a focused storage-shape subcomponent.
Actual slot mutation should be controlled by mutation/state-transition policy.
Slot storage maps to payload residency within slot.
Registry gap needs local registry subcomponent.
Container scan is not hot-path locating; allowed only as registry diagnostic inspection.
Registry mutation should be controlled by policy/guarded operation.
Registry rebuild requires its own safe-point guarded subcomponent.
Registry resolution should be a distinct indexed access concern.
Topology/scaling deserves a focused BundleAgent subcomponent instead of being mixed into the actor facade.
This must be implemented as a negative guard: waiting lists must not store envelopes.
Waiting list admission/consumption/rebuild should be captured in a lifecycle subcomponent.
Waiting list ordering/rebuild needs a policy subcomponent.
Round management gap belongs to membership/progression subcomponent.
Dynamic scheduling should be constrained into round policy, not a generic scheduler.
Locating/calculation should be isolated from access execution.
Reference pool should be separate from actor facade.
Reference supply behavior requires a subcomponent.
Actual ingress placement should be separated from the ingress facade.
Dispatch behavior/exposure needs execution subcomponent.
Copy/move semantics are external bridge-side contract tracked under dispatcher output contract.
Dispatch release/recycle needs a dispatcher release policy link.
Safe-point algorithms/execution require an evaluation/guard subcomponent.
Reset/recycle actual behavior needs execution subcomponent.
Eviction execution needs a safe-point guarded subcomponent beyond policy skeleton.
Bridge runtime concerns need a bridge-facing boundary subcomponent to track contracts without owning bridge internals.
Payload movement through boundary should be expressed as an exposure contract, not adapter runtime.
Read-side backpressure belongs under post-bridge profile.
Persistence pressure is post-bridge/downstream boundary within read-side profile tracking.
Read-side profile needs local profile subcomponent.
Receiver/read-side lifecycle belongs under post-bridge profile.
Read-side retry belongs under post-bridge profile.
